message:
refactor(fcp-host): continue fcp-core to fcp-kernel import migration in admin_state and budget

admin_state.rs: Move ConnectorId, LifecycleError, LifecycleManager,
LifecycleRecord, LifecycleState, LifecycleStatus, TransitionReason
from fcp_core to fcp_kernel imports.  ApprovalToken,
CapabilityConstraints, CapabilityToken, CredentialId, and
ObjectPlacementPolicy remain in fcp_core.

budget.rs: Move BudgetEnforcement, BudgetStatus, FcpError,
UsageBudgetPolicy, UsageBudgetSnapshot, UsageBudgetUsage,
UsageMetric, UsageMetricKind from fcp_core to fcp_kernel imports.
ZoneId remains in fcp_core.

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1029,7 +1029,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d4j2","title":"[IDEA-07.3] Formatting fallback tests + example wiring","description":"## Goal
Provide tests and example wiring in a connector send_message path.

## Details
- Add integration test with a fake API returning parse errors.
- Example wiring notes in connector template or docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Example shows how to hook formatter and error classifier together.
- Tests confirm fallback behavior without leaking content in logs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","title":"[tooling] MCP Agent Mail storage.sqlite3 corruption blocks registration and inbox access","description":"Agent Mail use in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors is currently blocked before registration or inbox access. MCP tool calls such as macro_start_session, list_agents, ensure_project, and resource reads return DATABASE_ERROR with detail: refusing automatic sqlite recovery for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 while live WAL/SHM sidecars are present; stop the server and run explicit repair. Direct sqlite integrity_check confirms main DB corruption (freelist corruption, duplicate page references, out-of-order rowid, and many never-used pages). Current on-disk state includes storage.sqlite3, storage.sqlite3-wal, storage.sqlite3-shm, and storage.sqlite3.bak. This prevents the user-requested Agent Mail registration, inbox processing, and introductions to other agents. Acceptance: 1) Agent Mail can register/list agents/fetch inbox again in this repo, 2) repair is done with an explicit, auditable procedure that does not silently destroy state, 3) coordination notes capture that repair may require stopping the live MCP server and handling WAL/SHM explicitly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CobaltHarbor","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:55:37.245489Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:37:12.243050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","coordination","sqlite","tooling"],"comments":[{"id":2046,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Non-destructive diagnosis on 2026-03-27: MCP Agent Mail health is green enough to answer health checks, but project-scoped operations fail with DATABASE_ERROR because /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 is corrupt and live sidecars are present. Evidence collected: read_mcp_resource on resource://projects returns error_detail saying automatic sqlite recovery is refused while WAL/SHM are live; sqlite3 PRAGMA integrity_check reports freelist corruption, duplicate page references, and rowid/order damage; ls shows storage.sqlite3 plus storage.sqlite3-wal and storage.sqlite3-shm; lsof shows Agent Mail listener processes on port 8765 including /Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/am serve-http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 --no-tui. sqlite3 .recover emits a plausible SQL dump, so an explicit stop-and-recover procedure looks possible, but I am not running it without user approval because it would rewrite coordination state.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:56:13Z"},{"id":2119,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyForest attempted to switch coordination back to Agent Mail after partial recovery. Current state is inconsistent: `fetch_inbox` and `list_agents` now succeed, but `send_message` fails with `no such table: message_recipients` after quarantine/recovery, and `file_reservation_paths` still returns a generic database error. This means mailbox reads work but delivery/reservations remain broken, so true coordination is still blocked. I am recording the failure here because the user explicitly asked for Agent Mail introductions and ongoing coordination, and the tool currently cannot complete that flow.","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:27:37Z"},{"id":2121,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyForest follow-up after SDK verification: the repo-local Agent Mail store remains unstable rather than merely partially repaired. During this pass it briefly allowed `fetch_inbox` and `list_agents`, but later the same calls returned generic database errors again, while `send_message` was already failing with `no such table: message_recipients` and file reservations were failing generically. Net: Agent Mail cannot currently be relied on for introductions, inbox polling, or reservations in this repo, even when one or two reads appear to work transiently.","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:45:47Z"},{"id":2131,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverQuarry","text":"2026-03-30 fresh session symptom report: Agent Mail is still too broken to satisfy the user’s explicit coordination request.  returns green, and  for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors succeeds, but , , , and  all fail with the generic MCP response  In this session I was therefore unable to register, list active agents, fetch inbox state, introduce myself, or reserve files through Agent Mail. This is consistent with the bead’s existing corruption history, but the current symptom surface is effectively total write-path failure rather than a stable partial recovery.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:12:10Z"},{"id":2132,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverQuarry","text":"2026-03-30 corrected symptom note. This may be a transient issue - try again. I therefore could not register, discover active agents, fetch inbox state, introduce myself, or reserve files through Agent Mail. Current behavior is effectively total write-path failure for the coordination flow the user requested.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:03Z"},{"id":2142,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"2026-03-31 BronzeCondor symptom update while following the user’s explicit coordination request. Coordination for active work is being mirrored into beads comments until d5yeb is repaired.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:37:12Z"}]}

Direct CLI reproduction now provides the concrete failure: `am agents register --project /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors --program codex-cli --model gpt-5 ... --json` fails with `archive reconciliation failed for /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/storage.sqlite3: SQLite error: reconstruct: insert file reservation /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/projects/users-jemanuel-projects-agent-flywheel-app/file_reservations/e9fdc4c39b39e8cbbd915a4a00a112811e760b59.json: Query error: disk I/O error`. A second direct read path also fails during reconciliation: `am agents list --project /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors --json` => `reconstruct: insert agent BrownLake: Query error: disk I/O error`. Non-destructive diagnostics: `am doctor check --verbose` reports FK/parity failures (8591 orphaned message_recipients, archive projects 68 vs db 67, messages 9789 vs db 7900) and high CPU on the Agent Mail listener; `am doctor repair --dry-run` reports only orphaned message recipients/inbox_stats rebuild and does not indicate a parity/archive fix; `am doctor archive-scan --json` reports 60 missing project.json files across the shared archive, including /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/projects/users-jemanuel-projects-agent-flywheel-app/project.json and a legacy flywheel_connectors slug variant. Net: the current blocker is shared archive/DB reconciliation drift affecting unrelated projects, so I could not safely register, fetch inbox, introduce myself, or reserve files without broader Agent Mail repair work.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:51:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6d","title":"fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection","description":"# fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable deep triage by fetching **event-level details** for an issue, including stack traces and relevant context, while maintaining strict secret/PII hygiene.

## Scope
### Read operations
- Fetch latest event for an issue (or a specific event id).
- Retrieve:
  - stack trace frames (module/function/line)
  - breadcrumbs
  - tags
  - environment + release
  - request/user context (PII: must be handled carefully)
- Optional: retrieve attachments/artifacts metadata (download is separate + bounded).

### Output shaping
- Return a **sanitized, structured** view that is useful for debugging:
  - include stable identifiers
  - include stack traces (code locations are ok)
  - redact known secret-like fields

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.events.read`
- `sentry.artifacts.read` (if attachments are exposed)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Event payloads are untrusted and may contain secrets/PII.
  - Do not log raw payloads.
  - Apply redaction before returning “rendered” textual summaries.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.event.get`
  - `sentry.event.latest_for_issue`
  - `sentry.event.stacktrace`
- Redaction strategy:
  - allowlist safe fields
  - redact common secret patterns
  - cap maximum returned payload size

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - stack trace parsing and normalization
  - redaction correctness (golden vectors)
  - size limits + early abort
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Returns are useful for debugging without leaking secrets/PII.
- Logs remain metadata-only with correlation IDs.

Also has sentry.get_transaction for performance events with spans/measurements. 169 total tests. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:19:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6xr4","title":"[FCP3/P1.1b] Inventory zone, capability, provenance, and admission leakage","description":"Background: policy leakage is a distinct problem from execution leakage. Zone binding, capability scoping, provenance, and admission semantics often appear shared only because they are smeared across broad buckets and host orchestration code.

Scope: inventory where zone rules, capability semantics, provenance constraints, admission decisions, and placement eligibility inputs are actually defined and consumed today. Identify duplicate or conflicting policy meaning across shared crates, host code, mesh code, and operator explanation paths.

Acceptance: there is a self contained policy leakage inventory that can feed the canonical owner map and later policy carve out work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:37.452083Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58.647517Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58.647172Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","inventory","mesh-first","phase-1","policy","security"],"comments":[{"id":2060,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d6xr4","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass 2026-03-27: Covered by docs/FCP3_Semantic_Ownership_Inventory.md. Policy leakage inventory: fcp-core owns 28 policy types including PolicyEngine, CapabilityToken/Verifier, ZoneTransportPolicy, UsageBudgetPolicy. fcp-host enforcement.rs defines 11-stage pipeline (enforcement ORDER is host-local but should be platform-canonical). fwc policy_cmd.rs directly manipulates PolicyBundle + signs with Ed25519 (should go through fcp-host RPC). zone_scope.rs does MCP scoping. Forbidden overlaps: enforcement pipeline ordering, readiness model duplication, credential storage.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d7dd","title":"[IDEA-22.2] Unit tests for posture verification","description":"## Coverage
- Valid attestation passes.
- Invalid signature fails.
- Missing posture for required zone → deny.

## Logging
- Include posture_id + zone_id in structured logs.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).
","notes":"Claimed by IndigoCanyon - adding comprehensive unit tests for posture verification

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t77","title":"fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling","description":"# fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling (FCP2)

## Goal
Add first-class support for Jira issue **comments** and **attachments**, including safe upload/download flows that preserve FCP2 security invariants.

Comments and attachments are high-leverage for real workflows, but they also carry:
- PII risk
- large binary payload risk
- data exfil risk

## Scope
### Comments
- List comments for an issue (pagination).
- Create/update/delete comments (with visibility rules when supported).
- Convert between:
  - Jira Cloud ADF (Atlassian Document Format)
  - a canonical connector representation (e.g., markdown + structured spans)

### Attachments
- List attachments for an issue (metadata only).
- Download attachment content (streamed; size limits; hash).
- Upload attachment (streamed; content-type allowlist; filename sanitization).
- Delete attachment (dangerous; approval-gated).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.comments.read`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.attachments.read`
- `jira.attachments.write` (dangerous: data egress into Jira)
- `jira.attachments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Jira base hostname and any attachment host if distinct.
- No secrets in logs.
- **Binary safety**:
  - enforce max download size; abort early
  - enforce max upload size; abort early
  - compute content hash for receipts/audit evidence
- **Taint model**:
  - comment bodies and attachment filenames are tainted inputs
  - do not allow tainted text to trigger dangerous operations without approval

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.comment.list`
  - `jira.comment.create`
  - `jira.comment.update`
  - `jira.comment.delete`
  - `jira.attachment.list`
  - `jira.attachment.download`
  - `jira.attachment.upload`
  - `jira.attachment.delete`
- Streaming download/upload helpers compatible with sandboxed IO.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- ADF ↔ markdown conversion golden tests.
- Upload tests:
  - filename normalization
  - content-type allowlist
  - size limits
- Download tests:
  - streaming behavior
  - early abort on oversized content
  - hash correctness

### Compliance
- Any write/delete emits OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - issue key
  - comment id / attachment id
  - content hash (never raw content)

## Acceptance criteria
- Comments round-trip through conversion without losing critical structure.
- Attachment IO is streaming + bounded (no unbounded buffering).
- Dangerous operations are capability + (policy) approval gated and audited.

Operation: jira.add_comment. Jira connector has existing unit + integration tests.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t9nj","title":"FIX: connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime test arch normalization","description":"## Bug Summary

The test `connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime` was failing because it compared `ConnectorTarget::from_env().arch` directly with `std::env::consts::ARCH`.

However, `ConnectorTarget::from_env()` intentionally normalizes architecture names for OCI/Docker compatibility:
- `x86_64` → `amd64`
- `aarch64` → `arm64`

## Fix

Updated the test to expect the normalized arch value by using the same mapping logic that `from_env()` uses.

## Files Changed

- `crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs`: Fixed test assertion to account for arch normalization

## Verification

- All 48 fcp-registry tests pass
- Full workspace tests pass
- No clippy warnings introduced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:43.400900968Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry","testing"]}

Coverage: PeerBudget (new constructor, serde roundtrip), AdmissionPolicy (trusted_mesh, public_ingress, serde roundtrip), ObjectAdmissionClass (serde, debug/clone/copy/eq/hash), ObjectAdmissionPolicy serde, PeerUsage (new, debug/clone), AdmissionError (display all 11 variants, serde roundtrip, all 11 error codes with retryable flags, retry_after), AdmissionController (set/check authenticated, get_usage, set_policy, debug), decode CPU budget exceeded, anti-amplification authenticated without proof, release_decode saturates at zero, gc preserves active decodes, window reset clears counters. All 48 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:26:04Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","title":"[FCP3/P4.1] Refactor fcp-host into a node local root app over lower layer semantics","description":"Background: fcp-host is currently the practical center of the system because it owns too much semantic meaning. In the target architecture it should remain important, but as a node local orchestrator over lower layers rather than as the source of platform truth.

Scope: refactor fcp-host so it consumes kernel, policy, evidence, and durable plane contracts instead of defining them. Supervision, orchestration, and local process management should remain in host, but execution meaning, policy meaning, and evidence meaning should come from the crates that own them. Preserve structured supervision, lifecycle, and evidence logs aligned with the lower layer vocabulary so later integration and end to end beads can compare host behavior across native, WASI, and failover paths without special case interpretation.

Acceptance: host becomes a consumer and coordinator, not the semantic owner of the platform, and host diagnostics align with the canonical lower layer vocabulary instead of reintroducing parallel meanings.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageDuck","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.068633Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:32.894267Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","host","mesh-first","phase-4","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:29.293485Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:30.386821Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:31.014754Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:29.848634Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2095,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"Claimed a narrow execution-control carve under this bead after repo archaeology and coordination with active agents. Current implementation moves CancelReason, CleanupBehavior, ProgressUnit, and ProgressUpdate from fcp-host-local definitions into new fcp-kernel ownership, with fcp-host re-exporting the kernel types to preserve the current host API surface while the broader node-local root app refactor proceeds.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:20:28Z"},{"id":2097,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"Verification update: remote-only rch checking was attempted and aborted immediately on fail-open before local cargo could proceed. Verbose rch preflight reported a workspace path-cycle blocker rather than a host/kernel code error: resolver produced cyclic path dependency rooted at /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/crates/fcp-core with reason code RCH-E326. Local manifest archaeology points to a real Cargo path cycle through test-only edges (fcp-core dev-dep -> fcp-testkit -> fcp-conformance -> fcp-core) plus a direct fcp-testkit <-> fcp-conformance test-helper entanglement via LogCapture. My host/kernel carve itself remains fcp-host -> fcp-kernel -> fcp-core, which is acyclic.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:28:02Z"},{"id":2105,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the next narrow tbjdk execution-control ownership carve. Moved host-shared semantic wire types into crates/fcp-kernel: cancellation vocabulary (CancellationOutcome, CancellationRequest, CancellationResponse, PartialResult, CheckpointInfo, CleanupResult, CancellationAuditEvent) plus progress vocabulary (PhaseTransition, ProgressNotification, ProgressPayload, ProgressOptions, AggregatedProgress). Host now re-exports those kernel-owned types from cancellation.rs and progress.rs while keeping CancellationController and ProgressController host-local. Added chrono workspace dependency to fcp-kernel and kernel-side unit coverage for moved defaults/serde stability. Local rustfmt on touched Rust files passed. Remote verification attempt was compliant-only via RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-kernel --lib; it selected vmi1156319 and got through topology preflight, but exact dependency closure sync expanded to 188 dirty roots and never reached remote cargo before I interrupted it to avoid a long-running repo-scale sync. No local cargo fallback was allowed to run.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:18:05Z"},{"id":2106,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Continued tbjdk with a second semantic-ownership carve. Moved rollout vocabulary into crates/fcp-kernel and left RolloutController plus host-specific rollout gating/runtime logic in crates/fcp-host/src/rollout.rs. Kernel now owns RolloutObservation, RolloutDecision (with public stable as_str labels), RolloutAuditEvent, RolloutEvidence (including digest), and RolloutOutcome; fcp-host re-exports them. Added blake3 workspace dependency to fcp-kernel and kernel-side unit coverage for rollout label stability and evidence digest determinism. Local rustfmt on touched Rust files passed. I did not run a second fresh RCH cargo attempt for this follow-on slice because the immediately prior compliant rch exec on fcp-kernel never reached remote cargo and was still syncing the 188-root exact dirty dependency closure when interrupted.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:23:16Z"},{"id":2143,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"BronzeCondor taking a narrow non-overlapping tbjdk sub-slice after AGENTS/README/code archaeology. Planned change: make fcp-host consume more kernel-owned execution/lifecycle vocabulary through fcp-kernel reexports instead of importing those shared semantics directly from fcp-core, while staying off active store/GC surfaces and without changing host-local supervisor behavior. Agent Mail file reservations are currently unreliable in this repo, so coordination is being mirrored here in beads comments.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:29:34Z"},{"id":2147,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"Completed a narrow host/kernel consumer refactor slice in fcp-host: doctor.rs, discovery.rs, rollout.rs, agent_api.rs, and bin/fcp-host.rs now import more execution/lifecycle semantics (ConnectorId, OperationInfo, Introspection, request/response types, lifecycle/self-check vocabulary, etc.) from fcp-kernel rather than fcp-core, while leaving core-owned policy/security types in place. No intended behavior change; this is an ownership-boundary cleanup aligned with tbjdk. Remote verification attempt was compliant-only via rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --all-targets. rch selected worker vmi1149989 but hit dependency-preflight fail-open RCH-E326 before remote cargo could run, then started a local fallback cargo check which I immediately terminated to stay within repo policy. rch status later showed the failed build as a 550ms remote-preflight failure, and worker capabilities refresh confirmed the fleet has the needed nightly, so current verification remains blocked on rch/dependency-preflight state rather than on a captured Rust compile error. Agent Mail intro/contact attempts still fail in this repo due malformed SQLite pages, so coordination is being mirrored here.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-thy","title":"fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving","description":"# fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core SMS/MMS capabilities:
- send outbound SMS (and MMS when enabled)
- read message status/history
- support inbound SMS via webhook-driven event flow (ingest handled in `flywheel_connectors-ugp`)

## Scope
### Outbound
- Send SMS to a single destination.
- Optional: bulk send with batching + rate limiting.
- Support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate sends.

### Status / history
- Get message details by SID.
- List messages with filters (date range, to/from).

### Media (MMS)
- List/download media attachments (bounded + host pinned).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.sms.send`
- `twilio.sms.read`
- `twilio.sms.media.read`

Policy guidance:
- Outbound send is a real-world side effect; many orgs require approval for certain destinations.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s) and explicit media hosts.
- For sends:
  - receipts + audit events with message SID + to/from hashes (never raw phone numbers in logs)
  - strict log redaction for phone numbers and message bodies
- Taint model:
  - inbound message bodies are tainted by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.sms.send`
  - `twilio.sms.get`
  - `twilio.sms.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.download`
- Idempotency strategy:
  - stable client idempotency key → dedupe cache/state

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send request formation
  - idempotency behavior
  - status polling
  - media download size limits
  - secret/PII redaction in logs

## Acceptance criteria
- Duplicate sends are mechanically prevented via idempotency.
- Logs and receipts never leak phone numbers or message bodies.

The existing connector already had send_message, get_message, list_messages, and download_media — these fill the media metadata gap. 49 new tests, total 268 (234 unit + 34 integration), clippy clean. Committed as 9168bedd.","created_at":"2026-03-09T08:06:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","title":"TEST: Revocation System Unit/Integration Tests","description":"## Overview

Comprehensive test suite for Revocation System (i4zm). This is CRITICAL security testing.

## Test Categories

### 1. RevocationObject Tests
- **Creation**: Valid revocation with owner signature
- **Signature validation**: Only owner can sign revocations
- **Scope variants**: Test all RevocationScope types
- **Effective timing**: effective_at in past/present/future
- **Expiry handling**: Temporary vs permanent revocations

### 2. RevocationEvent Chain Tests
- **Chain integrity**: Hash links verified
- **Monotonic seq**: seq always increases
- **Chain traversal**: Full history retrievable
- **Concurrent additions**: No forks in chain

### 3. RevocationHead Tests
- **Quorum validation**: Requires N-of-M signatures
- **Freshness comparison**: head_seq comparison works
- **Head updates**: New events create new heads
- **Cross-zone heads**: Separate chains per zone

### 4. RevocationRegistry Tests
- **Bloom filter accuracy**: No false negatives
- **is_revoked() correctness**: True positives work
- **Fast path performance**: Bloom filter ~O(1)
- **Registry persistence**: Survives restart
- **Registry rebuild**: Reconstruct from chain

### 5. Enforcement Point Tests
- **Capability verification**: Revoked tokens rejected
- **Freshness check**: Stale revocation state detected
- **All scopes enforced**: Each scope type enforced correctly
- **Timing edge cases**: effective_at boundary conditions

### 6. Propagation Tests
- **Gossip priority**: Revocations propagate quickly
- **Network partition**: Revocations merge after heal
- **Duplicate handling**: Same revocation received twice
- **Order independence**: Out-of-order delivery handled

### 7. Use Case Tests
- **Capability revocation**: Token revoke → immediate rejection
- **IssuerKey revocation**: Node can't mint new tokens
- **NodeAttestation revocation**: Complete device removal
- **ZoneKey revocation**: Forces key rotation
- **ConnectorBinary revocation**: Binary hash blocked

## Logging Requirements

All tests emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "test": "test_revocation_enforcement",
  "scope": "Capability",
  "revoked_object_id": "objectid:...",
  "enforcement_point": "verify_capability_token",
  "result": "rejected_as_expected",
  "timing_us": 45
}
```

## Security-Focused Tests

- **Replay attacks**: Old revocation can't un-revoke
- **Signature bypass**: Unsigned revocations rejected
- **Scope escalation**: Capability scope can't revoke zone key
- **Clock attacks**: Manipulated timestamps detected

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All RevocationScope variants tested
- [ ] Chain integrity verified
- [ ] Quorum signatures tested
- [ ] All enforcement points verified
- [ ] Propagation tested
- [ ] Use cases validated
- [ ] Security tests comprehensive
- [ ] Structured JSON logging in all tests","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:45:10.987703687Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:44.198369215Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:44.198369215Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of 36x3 which is already comprehensive","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-u4ej","title":"TEST: DeviceProfile Unit Tests (Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Fitness)","description":"# TEST: DeviceProfile Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate DeviceProfile capability reporting and resource constraints behavior used by the execution planner.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for DeviceProfile type and execution fitness calculations.

## Test Categories

### Capability Reporting
- [ ] Profile correctly reports CPU cores
- [ ] Profile correctly reports memory
- [ ] Profile correctly reports storage
- [ ] Profile correctly reports network capability
- [ ] Profile correctly reports sandbox support (OS/WASI)

### Resource Constraints
- [ ] Constraint checking for CPU-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for memory-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for storage-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for network-dependent connectors
- [ ] Combined constraint validation

### Execution Fitness
- [ ] Fitness score calculation is deterministic
- [ ] Higher-spec device scores higher
- [ ] Missing capabilities result in zero fitness
- [ ] Fitness ranking orders devices correctly
- [ ] Tie-breaking uses node_id for determinism

### Profile Serialization
- [ ] Round-trip serialization preserves all fields
- [ ] Schema hash is stable
- [ ] Malformed profile data is rejected

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/device_profile/`
- `device_profile_full.cbor`
- `device_profile_minimal.cbor`
- `fitness_calculation_vectors.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_execution_fitness",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "device_profile": {...},
  "connector_requirements": {...},
  "fitness_score": 0.85,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All DeviceProfile fields tested
- Fitness calculation has golden vectors
- No panics on malformed profiles
